title: Shinjis Impact in 20 and the Mark of the Beast
display_title: Shinjis Impact in 20 and the Mark of the Beast
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Impact in 2.0 and the Mark of the Beast             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Mar 12, 2014 8:35 pm Hi guys. I was rewatching 2.0 while cramming for a biggie tomorrow and I noticed a few particular things that I'd like to let you in on. It all starts from here. Mari opens Unit 02 up with a secret code that she proclaims as " Invert Mode" and " The Beast ". Ritsuko follows up with this and says that Mari has entered a negative plug depth. Nothing ventured, nothing gained. Indeed, what is this black magic? We've never seen a unit release like this before. Mind you, Mari also popped up completely unannounced. No one authorized her takeoff. She initiates her assault but is forced back without a left arm and with heavy damage to her side. (Jesus was similarly pierced in his side by Longinus with a lance) Skip ahead... Rei removes Mari from the battle and Mari stumbles through a wall with 02 into Shinji. They have a brief conversation and we receive a brief glimpse of her blood flowing out of 02 and against Shinji's left hand. Shinji doesn't appear to react to it and Mari decides to help him out by lifting him up on 02's right hand(since it's left arm is sleeping with the fish) to survey the scene. On the way up, Shinji tells himself that he mustn't pilot the Eva. He holds his left hand, palm up, against his forehead, while Mari tells Shinji to have more fun. Shinji studies his bloodied hand before ditching Mari to return to Nerv HQ to hop in 01. Let's pause here for a second. You're probably wondering what the fuck I'm talking about. Let's analyze this scene from a different perspective. Mari flipped a switch on 02 that she referred to as The Beast. The beast, in many contexts, particularly christianity, is the antithesis of a holy order. This makes sense - 02's humanity was sacrificed in order to remove it's limits. It's plug depth was dropped to the negatives. One can argue that, in a sense, Mari was sacrificing her soul for better piloting skills, in order to gain access to The Beast. This is the beast of the sea , taken from Wikipedia(note how 02's left arm was flung into the water). I bolded the parts you should take note of: "John saw the beast "rise up out of the sea, having seven heads(02 started with 4 eyes and when approaching Shinji, had 3) and ten horns(when 02 removes it's limits, the electronic screen viewing the transformation stops exactly at the 10th limit label), and upon his horns ten crowns, and upon his heads the name of blasphemy." (Revelation 13:1) The beast was like a leopard, with feet like the feet of a bear, and had a mouth like a lion. One of the heads of the beast had a mortal wound (when 02 first gets hit in the head, the scene is set up to look like Mari was KO'd) but the wound was healed (02 is still operational when speaking to Shinji works) causing people to wonder and follow the beast. (Shinji, after being shown the barren landscape and being convinced("seduced") by Mari) (Revelation 13:1-10) This is the beast of the earth , taken from the same page(note how Shinji was shown a desolate landscape before returning to Nerv HQ): This second beast comes out of the earth whose overall appearance is not described, other than having "two horns like a lamb" (clear physical attributes of 02), and speaking "like a dragon".[5] His purpose is to promote the authority of the first beast with the ability to perform great signs, even making fire come down out of heaven. This second beast is also called the false prophet.[6] He speaks like a dragon commanding the people of the Earth to make an image "to" the beast that was wounded by a sword. It is declared that anyone who does not worship the beast or its image would be killed.[7] The lamb-horned beast from the earth also causes all people to receive the mark of the beast "in their right hand or in their forehead." (Shinji holds his palm above his forehead in 02's hand and when 01 is activated wth Rei saved, it gains a third eye on it's forehead)[8] That's only the Christian aspect of things though. According to Kabbalah(and various other religious) traditions, the left side of the the Divine Emanation(and the left hand path) is the opposite of the path of holiness. (Wikipedia explains the Kaballah bit reallyyyyyy well: The "left side" of divine emanation is a negative mirror image of the "side of holiness" with which it was locked in combat. [Encyclopaedia Judaica, Volume 6, "Dualism", p. 244]. While this evil aspect exists within the divine structure of the Sefirot, the Zohar indicates that the Sitra Ahra has no power over Ein Sof, and only exists as a necessary aspect of the creation of God to give man free choice, and that evil is the consequence of this choice. It is not a supernatural force opposed to God, but a reflection of the inner moral combat within mankind between the dictates of morality and the surrender to one's basic instincts. So Shinji gets the literal blood of The Beast on his right palm, is convinced by Mari, piloting The Beast, to return to 01 and fight... and what happens? 01 loses it's left arm. Didn't we just go through this? (Note: Gendou's right side of his body is sprayed with blood. He's viewing the battle through his left side. Remember the left hand path?) Shinji can't accomplish anything with the right side path of holiness apparently. But hey, did you think those red eyes were for nothin'? (There are blood bubbles in the scene - yep, there's the blood pact.) Shinji can protect himself well enough with the right side path of holiness, but to do any damage - the first time we've seen an offensive AT field - he needs to spawn out his left side arm. Black magic I tell you! Even the offensive AT field is ring shaped, which goes against the design of an AT field. Definitely some black magic at work here. Some other things I'd like to note before I leave this theory as is. To add to the overwhelming ideas of Shinji following the left hand path, his entry plug on the inside is all red and chaotic when he berserks. 01's arm and offensive AT field is even all chaotically-coloured. It's not a holy colour at all... until he reaches in to save Rei. 01 reaches out towards Zeruel's core with it's right hand, symbolizing holy power. This is unsurprisingly the same time Shinji's entry plug changes from red to blue. The beast of the sea could also, perhaps, be a reference to the Wunder in Q, considering it rises from the sea and also bears the number 666. Asuka's 02 in Q also follows the description of the beast(this was mentioned in another thread so I'll leave that there). EDIT holy shit, no joke, right after I posted this, my laptop flicked off.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Mar 12, 2014 9:02 pm The circular AT field is interesting. It seems that more sides = more power when it comes to the planar manifestation of an AT field. A circle has both infinite sides and one side.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Mar 12, 2014 9:13 pm Wait you mean you never noticed that? That was one of the first things I noticed when I saw 2 for the first time... I immediately assumed a bunch of people here noticed it as well, since it was a pretty big scene. But yeah, it is weird how this one time where Shinji defies all Eva logic, his AT field not only behaves but manifests itself differently.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Mar 12, 2014 9:23 pm No, I didn't catch it. I haven't gone through certain scenes in Rebuild frame-by-frame looking for clues as I have with EoE. I'm waiting for the completion before I can build a total interpretation of the work. The left hand path point is interesting and Shinji's journey has so far been very left hand path (as it is in Eva) but it needs an ending before I can call it that.

[]:             Quote Post by SSJ Spartan01 » Wed Mar 12, 2014 9:25 pm Awesome analysis man. I'm sure I've heard this pointed out before but you went in depth enough to remind me of a couple interesting aspects of Eva beast mode symbolism. Especially your Revelations reference to the beast having the body of a leopard and the mouth of a lion. Guess that explains the kitty cat beast mode we saw in 3.0. Can't believe that eluded me, it seemed so obvious. nice.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Wed Mar 12, 2014 9:41 pm Thank you Spartan. It was quick but it was worth it. Chuckman, if my biblical allusions are completely off point, there is one thing I can at least rely on, and it's the left hand path. That is straight from Jewish lore and Eva is riddled with that type of lore. However, there has been something on my mind for some time about this whole ordeal... In Q, Asuka also pilots a "Beast" when she pilots 02. Just before it does it's animal thing, Asuka calls out "Code Triple 7." That means that she called out 777. The number of the Beast is 666. What I'm pointing to here is this: Fate of the beast and false prophet The beast and the false prophet gather the kings of the earth and their armies to prepare for war against "He who sits on a white horse" (Either Shinji or Gendou - "The King of Lilin"). The battle results in the beast being seized, along with the false prophet, where they are thrown alive into "the lake of fire". Those against the "King of Kings, and Lord of Lords" are killed and left for the fowls.[14] In the lake of fire, the beast and the false prophet are tormented day and night(Asuka's meeting with her angel?) forever and ever. (The floating debris at the end of 2 and the red sands at the end of 3 both resemble lakes of fire).[15] The dragon joins them a little more than a millennium later after the great war.[16] So... either Asuka's code triple 7 is a false prophet - she guides Shinji and Rei around at the end of Q, so who knows - or it could also represent this: The seven heads of the beast represent both seven mountains and seven kings, and the ten horns are ten kings who have not yet received kingdoms. Of the seven kings we are told that five have fallen, one is, the other has not yet come. We are also told that the beast itself is an eighth king who is the seventh ruler, and is going to perdition. We are also told that this beast "was, and is not; and shall ascend out of the bottomless pit, and go into perdition"[4] Ultimately this doesn't spell out much good for Asuka, I think... Edit There's also the chance that Shinji was driving the beast and that Kaworu represents the white horse(commanded by Gendou, so it sorta fits) that smited Shinji down because he almost went against "the King of Kings."

[]:             Quote Post by SSJ Spartan01 » Wed Mar 12, 2014 10:13 pm Concerning the 777 beast mode in 3.0, I know that Crowley wrote an occult book titled 777; that number referring to the descent path of the Tree of life from the spiritual planes to the mortal ones. I don't know if you referred to the transcendence of Shinji along the left hand path in terms of ascended being or descent, but I suppose we can both agree it being a state that's fallen from grace; hence, descent. I never liked looking too much into this stuff but I remember hearing this book discussed self-mutilation in it, probably as a means to condition oneself. Asuka did prompt Unit 02 to self destruct while in kitty cat beast mode if that holds any significance. I don't know if I'm on to something or if I'm looking too deeply into it but us Eva fans being- well, Eva fans, we'll never know the difference. haha

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Thu Mar 13, 2014 9:32 am SSJ Spartan01 wrote: Concerning the 777 beast mode in 3.0, I know that Crowley wrote an occult book titled 777; that number referring to the descent path of the Tree of life from the spiritual planes to the mortal ones. I don't know if you referred to the transcendence of Shinji along the left hand path in terms of ascended being or descent, but I suppose we can both agree it being a state that's fallen from grace; hence, descent. I never liked looking too much into this stuff but I remember hearing this book discussed self-mutilation in it, probably as a means to condition oneself. Asuka did prompt Unit 02 to self destruct while in kitty cat beast mode if that holds any significance. I don't know if I'm on to something or if I'm looking too deeply into it but us Eva fans being- well, Eva fans, we'll never know the difference. haha

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Mar 13, 2014 9:50 am unitM wrote: Shinji's been self-mutilating himself for awhile now as well.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Thu Mar 13, 2014 10:40 am ElMariachi wrote: What, wait when did Shinji self-mutilated himself?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Mar 13, 2014 1:49 pm ^ That's not self-mutilation, self-mutilation is to harm oneself for the sake of harming one-self. Shinji piloting the Eva isn't self-mutilation, because he doesn't pilot it to be harmed, but for his own reasons(gain his father's praise, protect people he cares for, try to gain redemption with the spears), sure piloting the Eva can be painful, and very often is, but it's a normal consequence of fighting. Following your logic, every soldiers would be committing self-mutilation by going into battle.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sun Mar 16, 2014 5:36 pm ElMariachi wrote: ^ That's not self-mutilation, self-mutilation is to harm oneself for the sake of harming one-self. Shinji piloting the Eva isn't self-mutilation, because he doesn't pilot it to be harmed, but for his own reasons(gain his father's praise, protect people he cares for, try to gain redemption with the spears), sure piloting the Eva can be painful, and very often is, but it's a normal consequence of fighting. Following your logic, every soldiers would be committing self-mutilation by going into battle.
